concise_definition: 3 out 2 in flex offense : Actions that mainly focus on screening and cutting near the vicinity of a corner area to generate high percentage scoring opportunities, initially by way of three players around the perimeter and two players near the low post areas.

sections.heading: What is 3 out 2 in flex offense in basketball?
sections.text:
3 out 2 in flex offense features actions that mainly focus on screening and cutting near the vicinity of a corner area to generate high percentage scoring opportunities, initially by way of three players around the perimeter and two players near the low post areas.

Related:3 Out 2 In Motion Offense&Flex Offense

sections.heading: How to understand the player roles and diagrams on this page
sections.text:
In reference to any diagrams below, each of the offensive players are generally represented by the standardbasketball positionsin number format, unless otherwise noted.

In essence, 1 is thepoint guard; 2 is theshooting guard; 3 is thesmall forward; 4 is thepower forward; 5 is thecenter.

Furthermore, in terms of a brief legend for the diagrams:

- Black Dot or Small Circle:The black dot or small circle represents the basketball.

- Solid Arrow:The solid arrow indicates movement or a cut on the basketball court.

- Dotted Arrow:The dotted arrow represents a basketball pass.

- Wavy Arrow:The wavy arrow indicates dribbling.

- T-Shaped Symbol:The T-shaped symbol signifies a screen, also known as a pick.

- Double pipe symbol:The double pipe symbol denotes handoff action.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in flex offense featuring a back screen?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in flex offense that features aback screenalong with additional screening actions.

sections.heading: Part 1: Back screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense demonstrating a back screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense demonstrating a back screen.]

To begin, 2 receives the ball from 1 and afterwards, 1 cuts to the basket via a back screen set by 4 near the middle of the high post area.

Following that, 1 can receive the ball again from 2 and score via a layup.

If 1 is not open, then 1 can cut through to the empty left corner while 4 pops out to receive the ball from 2.

sections.heading: Part 2: Flex and screen away
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense also showcasing an away screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense also showcasing an away screen.]

Next, 3 can receive the ball from 4 and as that happens, 5 can sprint across the lane to set a flex screen.

1 could then cut towards the basket, utilize that screen, receive the ball and score around the rim.

Moreover, as the flex action occurs, 2 could cut to the top via the away screen set by 4, receive the ball as an alternative option, and take the three-point shot if open.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in flex offense featuring down screen action?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in flex offense that featuresdown screensfollowed by the screen and cut action from the corner.

sections.heading: Part 1: Down screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense that demonstrates down screen action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense that demonstrates down screen action.]

To start, 3 receives the ball from 1. Afterwards, 1 cuts away to set a down screen with 4 as the recipient of it.

4 utilizes that screen and cuts up toward the top while 1 cuts out towards the left side corner following the screening action.

After that, 4 can receive the ball from 3 and take the three-point shot if that is open.

Alternatively, 4 can simply keep possession of the ball while 3 flattens out to the empty right side corner.

sections.heading: Part 2: Flex and screen the screener
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense showcasing flex and screen the screener action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense showcasing flex and screen the screener action.]

Next, 2 can receive the ball from 4 and as that occurs, 3 can cut to the basket via the flex screen set by 5.

Furthermore, 5 can cut to thehigh postarea by way of a down screen set by 4, which also completesscreen the screeneraction.

From there, 3 can receive the ball from 2 and score via a layup at the rim or alternatively, 5 could receive it and take the mid-range shot if that is available.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in flex offense featuring roll and replace action?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in flex offense featuringroll and replaceaction, which could result in scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: Part 1: Roll and replace
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense that demonstrates roll and replace action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense that demonstrates roll and replace action.]

To start, 1 dribbles toward the basket on the left side via theon-ball screenset by 5.

Moreover, as 1 begins dribbling, 3 cuts down to the empty adjacent corner. Also, after setting the screen, 5 rolls to the basket while 4 replaces at the top, previously occupied by 1.

From there, 5 could receive the ball from 1 and score via a layup or dunk. Alternatively, 4 could receive the ball and potentially take a three-point shot if that is open.

On the other hand, if 4 is not open to take the shot, then 5 could self-replace at the left side low post area while 1 occupies the left side wing, previously filled by 3.

sections.heading: Part 2: Flex screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense that demonstrates flex screen action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in flex offense that demonstrates flex screen action.]

Next, 2 receives the ball from 4 and as that occurs, 3 cuts to the basket via the flex screen set by 5.

Following that, 3 receives the ball from 2 and could score via a layup at the rim.
